dispatched_effort.fallback_reason: per-agent effort 'low' resolved but not applied: no in-session per-agent effort seam
per-agent effort 'low' resolved but not applied: no in-session per-agent effort seam

phase3_findings.description: strip_comment treats a shell parameter expansion's # as a comment introducer, deleting the rest of the line and hiding a real walk; guard reported audited 1 of 1 at exit 0 over an undeclared find "$ROOT"
_first_path_operand consumes a separated option value as the path operand, so grep -r --include '*.sh' NEEDLE "$ROOT" and find -maxdepth 2 "$ROOT" are silently unflagged
has_marker searches the raw line, so marker text inside a string literal exempts a real walk on that line
The entire shell arm has no fixture positive control; every #711 fixture is a .py file, so emptying scan_shell leaves the suite green
The dual.py fixture comment claims it exercises the per-line dedupe, which arm 1's short-circuit makes unreachable; AST arm 2 also has no positive control
CLAUDE.md's new bullet claims the guard turns the suite RED for 'any recursive-walk token', contradicted by the same diff's residual list and the shell arm's root-operand condition
The arm-2 inline comment inverts the condition under which the arm is reached (says 'only when the literal arm was satisfied by a marker')
docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md was not updated though the issue's Documentation Needed requires the baseline-uniqueness invariant and the new guard in the desk-time lint roster
Docstring 'closed by enumeration' sets omit ./ operands, keyword-only glob calls, head indirection ($GREP), bare find, and docstring prose
scan_file's literal-arm findings are discarded when the file also fails to parse
run.sh's 'the one candidate token literal that remains in this file' is a self-referential count of the PR-#553 rot class
Unguarded rm on probe_tmp's /dev/null sentinel; PM_STUB_BIN uses a bare mktemp -d rather than probe_tmp
The call-shape assertion is named for a negative claim (no assertion reads a raw walk) that a lower bound cannot establish
CONTRIBUTING.md says the marker must be on the walk's line while _marker_lines accepts it anywhere in a multi-line span
_pm_committed_baseline_count collapses a not-a-repository root onto the git-unavailable token and discards git's stderr
The idempotence assertion is close to vacuous over a pure stateless read
Intra-module data shapes are anonymous tuples of optionals (EnumerationError kind, _read pair, scan_file pair, fold_continuations triple) whose legality rules live only in docstrings
lib/test/lint-tree-enumeration.py is committed 100755 while its structural sibling lint-gh-api-repo-path.py is 100644

Phase 3.1.5 completeness critic ran: independent AST re-enumeration of the audited population (semantic call analysis, not the guard's token literals) found no filesystem walk the guard misses — all 18 .walk() calls are ast.walk, no os.scandir/fwalk exists, and no unmarked **/non-literal glob. The audit's matched set is a superset of the independent signal.

fix_decisions.claim_text: _pm_committed_baseline_count should emit a third not-a-repo token
fix_decisions.evidence: the governing acceptance criterion specifies exactly two non-count tokens and defines git-unavailable as the arm for a NON-ZERO git ls-files exit, which a non-repository root is; adding a third token would deviate from the AC, and the shipped behavior already fails closed with a non-numeric token

parking_evidence.basis: the AC defines the token set the finding proposes to widen
parking_evidence.source: issue #711 acceptance criteria, the git-unavailable and no-committed-baseline criteria

fix_decisions.claim_text: the idempotence assertion is near-vacuous and could be dropped
fix_decisions.evidence: the issue's Testing Strategy names 'State and idempotency: the guard is a pure read, and running it twice over the same tree yields byte-identical output' as a required coverage dimension, so dropping it would violate the spec of record

parking_evidence.basis: the issue's Testing Strategy mandates this coverage dimension
parking_evidence.failing_input: the guard run twice over one population

fix_decisions.claim_text: replace the anonymous tuple returns with NamedTuples/dataclasses
fix_decisions.evidence: a pure-refactor request with no acceptance criterion behind it and no observed defect; the fail-closed invariants it would express are already enforced by construction and are fixture-driven

parking_evidence.basis: no AC covers the intra-module data shapes and no defect was demonstrated
parking_evidence.failing_input: none demonstrated — the illegal (None, None) state is unreachable from the single producer

fix_decisions.claim_text: file mode 100755 differs from the sibling lint's 100644
fix_decisions.evidence: cosmetic; both are invoked as python3 <path> from run.sh and the new file additionally carries a shebang, so the executable bit is coherent on its own terms

parking_evidence.basis: cosmetic mode difference with no behavioral consequence on either invocation path
